stages.title: Site Layout
stages.narration: Stage 1. Site layout. Benchmarks, grid lines, footprint and layout strings establish the building at the site center before excavation.
stages.explanation: Benchmarks and grid marking establish reference for excavation and structural alignment.

stages.title: Excavation
stages.narration: Stage 2. Excavation. Trenches are cut to competent bearing stratum for continuous strip foundations.
stages.explanation: Excavation reaches competent bearing strata aligned to grid and wall centerlines.

stages.title: Foundation
stages.narration: Stage 3. Foundation. PCC blinding, strip footings and the foundation wall are constructed to transfer loads to soil.
stages.explanation: Foundation distributes loads to soil; PCC provides level working base and protects structural concrete.

stages.title: Structural Reinforcement
stages.narration: Stage 4. Structural reinforcement. Starter and vertical bars are anchored into the base for seismic continuity.
stages.explanation: Rebar continuity improves ductility and prevents wall separation under earthquakes.

stages.title: Columns / Primary Structure
stages.narration: Stage 5. Columns / primary structure. This system uses masonry with vertical reinforcement and RC bands as the primary seismic system.
stages.explanation: Primary stability comes from vertical bars + RC bands working with interlocking masonry.

stages.title: Plinth Beam / Base Structure
stages.narration: Stage 6. Plinth beam and DPC are installed to tie the base and prevent moisture rise.
stages.explanation: Plinth beam ties the foundation; DPC breaks capillary rise.

stages.title: Walls
stages.narration: Stage 7. Interlocking brick walls are laid course-by-course with correct bond and corner interlock.
stages.explanation: Interlocking masonry improves shear transfer and reduces mortar dependency.

stages.title: Openings
stages.narration: Stage 8. Door and window openings are detailed with correct frames and alignment checks.
stages.explanation: Openings require detailing for lintel support and seismic continuity.

stages.title: Lintel / Ring Beam
stages.narration: Stage 9. Lintel band and roof band (ring beams) are cast to provide horizontal continuity and box action.
stages.explanation: RC bands at opening and roof levels prevent separation and distribute lateral forces.

stages.title: Roof Structure
stages.narration: Stage 10. Roof trusses, purlins, bracing and connections are assembled on the wall plate.
stages.explanation: Roof framing transfers loads to walls; bracing stabilizes against wind and earthquake.

stages.title: Roof Cover
stages.narration: Stage 11. Roof sheets are installed with correct fasteners and overlaps to resist uplift.
stages.explanation: Roof cover completes the load path and provides weather protection.

stages.title: Finishes
stages.narration: Stage 12. Plaster, paint and waterproofing protect the masonry and improve durability.
stages.explanation: Finishes protect against moisture and weathering without compromising structural continuity.

stages.title: Completed Structure
stages.narration: Stage 13. Completed structure. Full load path is continuous from roof to soil with seismic bands and anchored vertical reinforcement.
stages.explanation: Completed resilient interlocking brick house ready for occupancy.

foundation.title: Foundation System
foundation.purpose: Transfers vertical loads from walls and roof to bearing soil.
foundation.loadMechanism: Strip footing spreads pressure; PCC provides uniform bearing plane.

footing.title: Strip Footing
footing.purpose: Spreads wall loads over a wider bearing area in competent soil.
footing.loadMechanism: Concrete strip under all load-bearing walls; 300 mm depth minimum.

plinth_beam.title: Plinth Beam
plinth_beam.purpose: Ties foundation elements and supports wall loads at 450 mm plinth height.
plinth_beam.reinforcement: 4–6 bars 12–16 mm with stirrups @ 150 mm c/c
plinth_beam.function: Reduces cracking, aligns wall base, anchors vertical bars

dpc.title: Damp Proof Course
dpc.purpose: Prevents capillary rise of moisture into wall masonry.
dpc.loadMechanism: Continuous membrane at plinth level; no structural load.

wall.title: Interlocking Brick Wall
wall.purpose: Primary load-bearing envelope — 230 mm thick, 3 m height.
wall.compression: Bearing stress distributed through interlock keys
wall.jointMechanism: Dry-stack keys reduce sliding between courses
wall.loadTransfer: Compression to lintel band and roof band

vertical_reinforcement.title: Vertical Reinforcement
vertical_reinforcement.purpose: Provides ductility and holds walls together under earthquake.
vertical_reinforcement.anchorage: L-bar into plinth beam with 480 mm development length
vertical_reinforcement.seismicRole: Connects footing to lintel and roof bands; prevents corner separation

lintel_band.title: Lintel Band
lintel_band.purpose: Horizontal tie at opening level resisting wall separation during seismic shaking.
lintel_band.earthquakeMechanism: Continuous 150 mm RC ring beam at 2.1 m keeps walls acting as a box

roof_band.title: Roof Band
roof_band.purpose: Seismic tie at wall top creating box action with lintel band.
roof_band.boxAction: Walls cannot move independently; inertia forces shared through diaphragm

roof_structure.title: Roof Structure
roof_structure.purpose: Timber trusses, purlins and bracing at 15° slope.
roof_structure.loadMechanism: Trusses span 6 m width; purlins transfer sheet loads to trusses

roof_truss.title: Roof Truss
roof_truss.purpose: Primary roof framing element spanning building width.
roof_truss.loadMechanism: Bottom chord in tension; rafters carry purlin and sheet loads

roof_cover.title: Roof Covering
roof_cover.purpose: Metal sheets completing the roof diaphragm.
roof_cover.loadMechanism: Sheets span between purlins; wind uplift resisted by fixings
